articles.title: Jaipur’s SMS Hospital opens dedicated heatwave ward amid rising temperatures - The News Mill
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Rajasthan
articles.full_text:
Hospitals in Rajasthan are establishing dedicated wards and forming specialised teams to manage health issues caused by the current severe heatwave across the state. Jaipur’s SMS Hospital, the largest government hospital and medical college in Rajasthan, has implemented special arrangements to treat patients affected by extreme heat conditions.
Dr Deepak Maheshwari, Principal and Controller of Jaipur SMS Hospital and Medical College, told ANI: “There are two things. First, regarding the arrangements we have made, when the heat is at its peak, and patients start coming in (though not many cases have arrived yet), we have identified a separate ward and a separate ICU for this. We have a nodal officer and a dedicated team for this purpose. So, we have made those arrangements. As of yesterday, I checked, and no such heatstroke cases have come in yet. So, I don’t think there will be any problem. We have designated an ICU.”
Several locations in North India are experiencing intense heatwave conditions. On Wednesday, Jaipur recorded a maximum temperature of 41 degrees Celsius. The World Meteorological Organization defines a heatwave as five or more consecutive days during which the daily maximum temperature exceeds the average maximum temperature by five degrees Celsius. On Wednesday, the temperature departure from the normal maximum was below two degrees Celsius in most places, except in Chittorgarh, which registered a deviation of at least 2.5 degrees.
Dr Maheshwari shared vital precautions for the public to avoid heat-related illnesses: “As for the general public, I consistently advise two or three things. First, those in extreme age groups, the more vulnerable ones like children and the elderly, should not go out in the sun. They should avoid being out when the temperature is at its peak. If they must go out, they should wear full-sleeved clothes, cover their bodies, and carry an umbrella for shade. Third, they must stay well-hydrated. Drink plenty of water, coconut water, or other fluids. If these three things are followed, I believe 70% of cases are preventable.”
He added, “The elderly, children, and those who are weaker, meaning the elderly, children, and people with multiple underlying diseases or those who are already unwell, need to be particularly careful. Others should also take precautions, but these groups are more vulnerable and may face more difficulties.”
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast that heatwave conditions will worsen in parts of northwest India, with rising temperatures expected in West Rajasthan in the coming days. Additionally, hot and humid weather will prevail across several coastal areas, with isolated warm night conditions predicted in central India.
The IMD noted that heatwave conditions are very likely in isolated parts of Vidarbha on April 29, followed by a prolonged spell over West Rajasthan from May 1 to May 3.

Jaipur (Rajasthan) [India], April 30 : Hospitals in Rajasthan are setting up dedicated wards and making teams to deal with the incoming cases of illness due to the spell of intense heatwaves across the state. Special arrangements have been made at Jaipur's SMS Hospital, Rajasthan's largest government hospital and medical college, to care for patients bearing the scorching heat and such conditions.
According to Dr. Deepak Maheshwari, Principal and Controller of the Jaipur SMS Hospital and Medical College, speaking to ANI, said, "There are two things. First, regarding the arrangements we have made, when the heat is at its peak, and patients start coming in (though not many cases have arrived yet), we have identified a separate ward and a separate ICU for this. We have a nodal officer and a dedicated team for this purpose. So, we have made those arrangements. As of yesterday, I checked, and no such heatstroke cases have come in yet. So, I don't think there will be any problem. We have designated an ICU."
As various places across North India are facing an intense spell of heatwave, Jaipur on Wednesday recorded a maximum temperature of 41 degrees Celsius. The World Meteorological Organization defines a heat wave as five or more consecutive days during which the daily maximum temperature exceeds the average
maximum temperature by five degrees Celsius.
Departure from the maximum 'normal temperature' remained below 2 degrees Celsius in most places on Wednesday, except in Chittorgarh, which saw atleast a deviation of 2.5 degrees.
Dr. Deepak shares essential precautions, such as avoiding peak sunlight and staying hydrated, to help the general public prevent the majority of heat-related illnesses.
"As for the general public, I consistently advise two or three things. First, those in extreme age groups, the more vulnerable ones like children and the elderly, should not go out in the sun. They should avoid being out when the temperature is at its peak. If they must go out, they should wear full-sleeved clothes, cover their bodies, and carry an umbrella for shade. Third, they must stay well-hydrated. Drink plenty of water, coconut water, or other fluids. If these three things are followed, I believe 70% of cases are preventable," he added.
He further stated, "The elderly, children, and those who are weaker, meaning the elderly, children, and people with multiple underlying diseases or those who are already unwell, need to be particularly careful. Others should also take precautions, but these groups are more vulnerable and may face more difficulties."
Meanwhile, Heatwave conditions are set to intensify in parts of northwest India, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) warning of rising temperatures in West Rajasthan in the coming days, alongside hot and humid weather across several coastal regions and isolated warm night conditions in central India.

articles.title: Rajasthan Weather Update: भीषण गर्मी के बीच आंधी और बारिश का डबल अटैक, चित्तौड़गढ़ में 43 के पार पहुंचा पारा - Amar Ujala

articles.full_text:
Rajasthan Weather Update: भीषण गर्मी के बीच आंधी और बारिश का डबल अटैक, चित्तौड़गढ़ में 43 के पार पहुंचा पारा
भीषण गर्मी के बीच राजस्थान में अचानक बदले मौसम ने लोगों को राहत और परेशानी दोनों दी है। आंधी-बारिश के चलते कुछ जगह तापमान गिरा, तो कहीं तेज हवाओं ने जनजीवन प्रभावित कर दिया। मौसम विभाग ने अगले 48 घंटों के लिए कई इलाकों में अलर्ट जारी किया है।
विस्तार
राजस्थान में इन दिनों मौसम का दोहरा रंग दिखाई दे रहा है। एक ओर जहां भीषण गर्मी का असर जारी है, वहीं दूसरी ओर आंधी और बारिश ने कई जिलों में राहत दी है और कहीं-कहीं परेशानी भी बढ़ा दी है। बीते 24 घंटों में चित्तौड़गढ़ में अधिकतम तापमान 43.4 डिग्री सेल्सियस दर्ज किया गया, जबकि जयपुर सहित कई जिलों में मेघगर्जन के साथ हल्की बारिश हुई।
मौसम विभाग के अनुसार पश्चिमी विक्षोभ के सक्रिय होने से आगामी 48 घंटों के लिए जयपुर, अलवर, भरतपुर और सीकर समेत 15 जिलों में आंधी-बारिश का येलो अलर्ट जारी किया गया है। इस दौरान 30 से 50 किमी प्रति घंटे की रफ्तार से तेज हवाएं चलने की संभावना है। किसानों को फसल सुरक्षित रखने और आमजन को सावधानी बरतने की सलाह दी गई है।
गर्मी का असर बरकरार
राज्य के पश्चिमी हिस्सों में गर्मी का प्रकोप जारी है। जैसलमेर में 42.6°C, बाड़मेर में 42.0°C, फलौदी में 41.8°C और बीकानेर में 41.4°C तापमान दर्ज किया गया। बाड़मेर, जैसलमेर और जालौर में कहीं-कहीं लू की स्थिति बनी हुई है, जिससे जनजीवन प्रभावित हो रहा है।

जयपुर में बदला मौसम
राजधानी जयपुर में अधिकतम तापमान 41.0°C और न्यूनतम 28.7°C दर्ज किया गया। यहां आंशिक बादल छाए रहने, मेघगर्जन, वज्रपात और हल्की बारिश के साथ तेज हवाएं चलने की संभावना जताई गई है।
भरतपुर में राहत, कोटा में परेशानी
भरतपुर में सुबह तेज आंधी और झमाझम बारिश से मौसम सुहावना हो गया और तापमान में गिरावट दर्ज की गई। वहीं कोटा में तेज आंधी ने अव्यवस्था फैला दी। शाम के समय अचानक आए अंधड़ से शादी समारोह प्रभावित हो गए, टेंट उखड़ गए, लाइटिंग बंद हो गई और व्यवस्थाएं बिगड़ गईं।
आगे भी जारी रहेगा असर
मौसम विभाग का कहना है कि पश्चिमी विक्षोभ और बंगाल की खाड़ी से आने वाली हवाओं के प्रभाव से अगले 3-4 दिनों तक प्रदेश के कई हिस्सों में थंडरस्टॉर्म गतिविधियां जारी रहेंगी। 2 मई से एक और सिस्टम सक्रिय होने की संभावना है, जिससे आंधी की तीव्रता और बढ़ सकती है।
